name: Khalil Center
mission: Our wellness centers integrate Islamic spiritual approaches into professional psychology to help achieve optimal, holistic health by providing a wide variety of mental health services.
theoryOfChange: Khalil Center utilizes the Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) framework, which is a therapeutic model developed by their leadership. This framework integrates professional psychology into an Islamic context, outlining Islamic beliefs regarding health, pathology, human ontology, and epistemology. It provides a refined integrative approach within an Islamic framework for psychotherapeutic practice and guides their services and research.
scoreSummary: Khalil Foundation shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Practice-based evidence study published in 'Spirituality in Clinical Practice' (2023)
Research published in the Journal of Muslim Mental Health

all_citations.claim: The organization reported a program expense ratio of 71.2% and total revenue of $2,505,061.

Financial records indicate a fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised and working capital of 2.4 months.
The center provides clinical appointments and trains professionals in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP).
The organization tracks lifetime psychotherapy sessions delivered and hosts various support groups.
The charity provides multiple giving methods for donors to support its clinical mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its specialized clinical impact and excellent fundraising efficiency, balanced against limited financial reserves and a medium data confidence level.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with only 2.4 months of working capital available
High cost per beneficiary relative to large-scale global health interventions

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity aligns well with donors seeking specialized mental health interventions within a Muslim context, addressing a high-urgency global health need [2][6]. With total revenue of $2,505,061, it maintains a consistent track record of service delivery, though its medium donor fit score reflects the niche nature of its clinical model [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates a commitment to capacity building by training professionals in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) and delivering thousands of psychotherapy sessions [4][5]. While the program expense ratio is solid at 71.2%, the organization maintains a lean 2.4 months of working capital, and the cost per beneficiary is approximately $907.87 [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Khalil Foundation provides spiritually-integrated mental health services and clinical psychotherapy through a community-centered framework.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent to raise every $1
Strong programmatic focus with 71.2% of expenses dedicated to clinical and educational services
Clear zakat-eligibility policy for supporting mental health access
baseline_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, delivers clinical mental health services and community education that integrate psychological science with Islamic theology [1][4]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to support mental health treatment for those in need through specific asnaf categories [2].

alignment.improvement: The center could <cite id="28">clarify its zakat allocation policy</cite> to provide donors with more transparency on how funds are distributed across clinical cases.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that spiritual integration does not compromise clinical efficacy.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="11">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The center provides high-volume care, recording <cite id="12">13,065 clinical appointments</cite> in a single reporting year.
The organization maintains high levels of oversight with a board consisting of <cite id="13">100% independent members</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Khalil Center's primary strength lies in its specialized clinical model, which integrates traditional Islamic healing with modern psychotherapy. This is evidenced by their <cite id="14">TIIP training program</cite>, which has equipped hundreds of practitioners to serve Muslim populations globally. Furthermore, the organization maintains a <cite id="15">strong programmatic focus</cite>, with nearly 80% of its budget dedicated to its core mission of mental health and education.
rich_narrative.summary: The Khalil Foundation, operating as the Khalil Center, is a community-centered psychological and spiritual wellness center that provides <cite id="1">spiritually-integrated mental health services</cite> and clinical psychotherapy. Founded in <cite id="2">2010</cite>, the organization addresses the unique mental health needs of the Muslim community by bridging the gap between <cite id="3">psychological science and Islamic theology</cite>. The center has demonstrated significant scale in its operations, reporting <cite id="4">70,136 total psychotherapy sessions delivered</cite> over its lifetime and hosting <cite id="5">52 support groups</cite> to foster community resilience. Financially, the organization manages a budget of <cite id="6">$2,272,898 in total revenue</cite>, with a significant portion of its income derived from <cite id="7">$1,476,207 in program service revenue</cite>, indicating a sustainable model of earned income alongside charitable contributions. The organization maintains a <cite id="8">71.2% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that the majority of funds are directed toward clinical and educational initiatives. Beyond direct care, the Khalil Center serves as a hub for professional development, having <cite id="9">trained 500 professionals</cite> in Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP). This dual focus on direct service and <cite id="10">capacity building</cite> allows the organization to influence the broader field of Islamic psychology while providing immediate relief to individuals in crisis.
